heading: Payments to eligible States for incarceration of criminal aliens
label: 12110 Payments to eligible States for incarceration of criminal aliens
notes: References in Text, referred to in subsecs. (a) to (c), was redesignated by , , .Section 1252(j) of title 8section 1231(i) of title 8Pub. L. 104–208, div. C, title III, § 306(a)(1)110 Stat. 3009–607Sept. 30, 1996
CodificationSection was formerly classified to , The Public Health and Welfare, prior to editorial reclassification and renumbering as this section.section 13710 of Title 42
amendments: References in Text, referred to in subsecs. (a) to (c), was redesignated by , , .Section 1252(j) of title 8section 1231(i) of title 8Pub. L. 104–208, div. C, title III, § 306(a)(1)110 Stat. 3009–607Sept. 30, 1996

children.heading: In general
children.label: a In general
children.text: The Attorney General shall make a payment to each State which is eligible under section 1252(j) of title 8 in such amount as is determined under section 1252(j) of title 8, and for which payment is not made to such State for such fiscal year under such section.11See References in Text note below.1

children.heading: Authorization of appropriations
children.label: b Authorization of appropriations
children.text: Notwithstanding any other provision of this part, there are authorized to be appropriated to carry out this section from amounts authorized under , an amount which when added to amounts appropriated to carry out section 1252(j) of title 8 for fiscal year 1996 equals $500,000,000 and for each of the fiscal years 1997 through 2000 does not exceed $650,000,000.section 12108 of this title1

children.heading: Administration
children.label: c Administration
children.text: The amounts appropriated to carry out this section shall be reserved from the total amount appropriated for each fiscal year and shall be added to the other funds appropriated to carry out section 1252(j) of title 8 and administered under such section.1

children.heading: Report to Congress
children.label: d Report to Congress
children.text: Not later than , the Attorney General shall submit a report to the Congress which contains the recommendation of the Attorney General concerning the extension of the program under this section.May 15, 1999
